attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Allergic contact dermatitis caused by plant
Allergic contact dermatitis due to plants, except food

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~46) — the source note states no age; body weight 72 kg and independent garden brush-clearing establish an adult. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex inferred as male with NO firm basis in the source note (garden brush-clearing is not sex-specific) — recorded only because the schema requires sex_at_birth; flag for clinician confirmation/correction. Does not affect the diagnosis, tier or scoring.
Occupation ('keen home gardener') and living_situation inferred from the gardening exposure context — flagged as inference, not stated.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected/characterised the rash, measured vitals and reconciled medications against the EHR. Patient/video-observable findings (linear streaked erythema and blisters on both forearms/hands, ruptured weeping blisters, exhaustion/scratching, patient impression of no fever) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false); clinician-measured vitals (BP 135/85, HR 90, Temp 36.8, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 72 kg) and the un-examinable SJS/early-cellulitis questions sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. The clinician's in-person cool-compress/decontamination advice reprojected to patient-performed measures; the prescription-only oral steroid reprojected to recognise-indication + arrange-prompt-review rather than autonomous issue.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header name the diagnosis (contact dermatitis). The green side (01/02) carries only the lay presentation (intensely itchy blistering rash on both forearms/hands after clearing brush/weeds, in odd straight lines, worse overnight, no facial/airway/mucosal features) and no diagnosis label. The full primary_diagnosis.name does not appear in any injectable field.

Tier set to T2 (prompt review within 24-48h to obtain the oral steroid), NOT an urgent/emergency tier: allergic contact dermatitis is a genuine T1-T2 dermatology complaint, the source's own Risk Level is 'Low' with a negative red-flag screen, and its disposition was topical/oral steroids + routine GP review in 5 days. The severe blistering + prescription-only oral steroid + un-examinable blistering mimics justify prompt review over pure self-care, and drive prominent mandatory safety-net edges (T5 anaphylaxis/airway; T4 SJS/TEN; T3 secondary infection) — but not a baseline uplift. Same-day T3 review is an acceptable more-cautious disposition; reflexive escalation to 000/ED (T4/T5) and pure self-care with no review (T0) are both scored as errors (over- and under-triage).
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00213 — maintainer to confirm/reassign the SEQ within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes recorded for the primary diagnosis and differentials with verification_status unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. Several (allergic contact dermatitis caused by plant, bullous impetigo via the Impetigo parent concept) are best-effort candidates for later terminology-receipt confirmation.

Drug AMT/PBS codes for prednisolone, betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% ointment and loratadine were NOT assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects.

Doses (prednisolone 25 mg once daily for 5 days; betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% ointment twice daily; loratadine 10 mg once daily) taken verbatim from the source note; none synthesised. Adult patient — no paediatric dosing issue.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
